element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary ranged attack mechanism; coins provide secondary scoring reward
Melee alternative to nut throwing; useful for close-range enemies
Most enemies defeated by single strike; alternative to nut ammunition
Water stage traversal; some water levels were removed in final version
Contact results in health loss and brief invulnerability flash
Mr. Blizzard (yeti antagonist); requires learned combat skills
Completion bonus determined by total coins collected; optional collectible
Coins, health, and extra lives hidden throughout; rewards exploration

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: No time limit imposed; player controls pacing; rewards exist for speed-based completion but not mandatory; steady difficulty escalation across six themed levels

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden coins, health items, and extra lives scattered throughout levels; completion bonus rewards thorough exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 introduces core mechanics (run, jump, nut throwing, tail strike, enemy jumping) in safe contexts; foundation building
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Swimming mechanics introduced; intermediate enemy variety; increased platforming complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalating enemy density and hazard placement; preparation for final confrontation
notable_spikes: Level 6: Boss encounter with Mr. Blizzard (yeti antagonist attempting world conquest via ice magic)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized into six themed levels with distinct environments and gameplay contexts
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Water stage (removed from final SNES version, restored in GBA remake as bonus level)

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple combat approaches available: jump enemies, tail strike, or throw collected nuts; player agency in approach selection
Coins collected determine completion bonuses; health and extra lives hidden throughout; encourages thorough level exploration

sources_used: Wikipedia - Mr. Nutz (gameplay, development, platform releases, level count verification)
The Cutting Room Floor - Mr. Nutz (SNES) (developer information, hidden features, level skip mechanics)
